## Module Boundaries

- Each skill directory is an independently consumable Codex skill bundle.
- `util_skills/` contains maintenance and generation skills rather than research-paper workflow skills.
- `references/` holds longer task guidance loaded only when needed.
- `scripts/` holds reusable automation entry points.
- `templates/` and `assets/` hold files copied or adapted into generated downstream projects.
- `agents/openai.yaml` files are per-skill agent metadata/configuration surfaces.

## Entry Points

- `training-code-architecture-skill/scripts/create_project.py`
- `latex-paper-build-skill/scripts/create_paper_pipeline.py`
- `latex-paper-build-skill/scripts/scaffold_latex_paper.py`
- `util_skills/project-agent-generator-skill/scripts/generate_project_agents.py`

## Tests / Validation

- No in-repository test suite detected.
- README documents validation through `quick_validate.py` for individual skill folders.
- README also recommends `python -m py_compile <script.py>` when scripts are added or changed.

## Generated / Output Areas

- None detected at top level.
- `.agent/` is generated agent context for this repository and may be regenerated with the project-agent generator when the user requests it.

## Boundary Notes

- Avoid changing skill trigger text, folder names, or frontmatter without checking README consistency.
- Treat templates/assets as source material unless the user explicitly asks to regenerate them.
